text: Hubei's mobility network is centered on Wuhan, with Xiangyang and Yichang acting as major sub-centers.
Xiaogan, Jingzhou, and Huanggang can be interpreted as potential nodes because they connect secondary flows to the Wuhan core area.
A railway station opening can be evaluated by comparing observed migration flow against a counterfactual baseline.
Synthetic control creates a weighted counterfactual path from comparable city pairs before policy implementation.
Future planning scenarios can estimate how investment intensity changes inter-city mobility demand.
Spatio-temporal graph convolution can model both temporal trends and network adjacency when forecasting mobility flows.
Louvain community detection is useful for identifying cohesive urban clusters in a migration network.
Changing the community-resolution parameter reveals city-network patterns at provincial, regional, and corridor scales.
A planning report should connect model outputs to transport, industry, and public-service allocation recommendations.
The Agent should convert natural-language planning questions into explicit tool calls, observe tool outputs, and then produce a grounded answer.
RAG grounding reduces unsupported conclusions by retrieving relevant planning knowledge before the final response.
The frontend demo presents maps, rankings, policy controls, scenario sliders, and AI-style narrative reports for portfolio review.
